channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: Elon Musk boosts an X post by Ronan Farrow promoting his New Yorker article on Sam Altman's alleged deceptions, as Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI heads to trial (Wired)
item.description: Wired : Elon Musk boosts an X post by Ronan Farrow promoting his New Yorker article on Sam Altman's alleged deceptions, as Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI heads to trial   —  The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

item.title: Jury selection complete in Musk v. Altman trial; opening arguments begin Tuesday in Oakland
item.description: A nine-person jury was seated Monday in the federal Musk v. Altman trial in Oakland, California; both Sam Altman and Greg Brockman attended. Opening statements and witness testimony begin Tuesday. The civil case centres on whether OpenAI's shift to a for-profit structure breached its founding charter, with Musk, Altman, and possibly Satya Nadella expected to testify over four weeks.

item.title: NZ mid-sized firms lag on AI readiness, MYOB finds
item.description: MYOB's annual readiness survey shows New Zealand's mid-market is investing in AI but trailing on workforce skills, governance, and process autonomy. Useful baseline for any NZ corporate AI conversation in 2026.

item.title: Tracking the history of the now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause
item.description: For many years, Microsoft and OpenAI's relationship has included a weird clause saying that, should AGI be achieved, Microsoft's commercial IP rights to OpenAI's technology would be null and void. That clause appeared to end today. I decided to try and track its expression over time on openai.com . OpenAI, July 22nd 2019 in Microsoft invests in and partners with OpenAI to support us building benef

item.title: Speech translation in Google Meet is now rolling out to mobile devices
item.description: Speech translation in Google Meet is now rolling out to mobile devices I just encountered this feature via a "try this out now" prompt in a Google Meet meeting. It kind-of worked! This is Google's implementation of the ultimate sci-fi translation app, where two people can talk to each other in two separate languages and Meet translates from one to the other and - with a short delay - repeats the t

item.title: GitHub says all Copilot plans will move to usage-based billing on June 1, replacing premium requests with monthly GitHub AI Credits (Mario Rodriguez/The GitHub Blog)
item.description: Mario Rodriguez / The GitHub Blog : GitHub says all Copilot plans will move to usage-based billing on June 1, replacing premium requests with monthly GitHub AI Credits   —  Starting June 1, your Copilot usage will consume GitHub AI Credits.  —  TL;DR: Today, we are announcing that all GitHub Copilot plans will transition to usage-based billing on June 1, 2026.

item.title: The EU unveils new proposals under the DMA aimed at opening up Android to rivals' AI services; Google says the measures are "unwarranted intervention" (Samuel Stolton/Bloomberg)
item.description: Samuel Stolton / Bloomberg : The EU unveils new proposals under the DMA aimed at opening up Android to rivals' AI services; Google says the measures are “unwarranted intervention”   —  Google was targeted by European Union watchdogs who unveiled a slate of proposals aimed at prising open its Android ecosystem to rivals' AI services.

item.title: 600+ Google and DeepMind employees sign open letter demanding Pichai bar DOD from using Google AI for classified military work
item.description: More than 600 Google and DeepMind employees signed a letter to CEO Sundar Pichai demanding he reject classified AI contracts with the US Department of Defense as Google negotiates to deploy Gemini in Pentagon settings. Over 100 DeepMind researchers separately sent an internal letter barring use of DeepMind research in weapons development or autonomous targeting.

item.title: The founder of car rental platform PocketOS says a Cursor agent using Claude Opus 4.6 accidentally deleted a production database while in a staging environment (Jer/@lifeof_jer)
item.description: Jer / @lifeof_jer : The founder of car rental platform PocketOS says a Cursor agent using Claude Opus 4.6 accidentally deleted a production database while in a staging environment   —  An AI Agent Just Destroyed Our Production Data. It Confessed in Writing.

item.title: Andrew Ng: AI-native software engineering teams operate very differently
item.description: Andrew Ng essay on how AI-native engineering teams collapse the engineer/PM ratio, why generalists win, and how coding agents shift bottlenecks to design, marketing, and legal. Useful frame for hotloop-style team design conversations.

item.title: Canva Magic Layers silently replaced 'Palestine' in user designs; company says issue is fixed
item.description: Canva's newly launched Magic Layers feature automatically replaced the word 'Palestine' in user designs, sparking a viral backlash on X. Canva said it 'moved quickly to investigate and fix' the issue. The incident highlights risks of AI content moderation embedded directly in creative tools.

item.title: Ex-DeepMind Principal Scientist David Silver raises $1.1B seed at $5.1B valuation for Ineffable Intelligence
item.description: David Silver, who led DeepMind's reinforcement learning team and co-created AlphaZero, has raised $1.1B at a $5.1B valuation for UK startup Ineffable Intelligence — the largest seed round in European history. The lab aims to build AI 'superlearners' that acquire knowledge without human data via RL. Backers include Sequoia, Lightspeed, NVIDIA, Google, DST Global, and the UK Sovereign AI Fund.

item.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
item.description: OpenAI is available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling secure AI adoption for U.S. federal agencies.

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI remove a clause that would have given Microsoft IP rights until OpenAI achieved "AGI"; Microsoft retains use of OpenAI's models until 2032 (Aaron Holmes/The Information)
item.description: Aaron Holmes / The Information : Microsoft and OpenAI remove a clause that would have given Microsoft IP rights until OpenAI achieved “AGI”; Microsoft retains use of OpenAI's models until 2032   —  Microsoft and OpenAI amended the terms of their arrangement, allowing OpenAI to sell its models on competing cloud providers, the companies said on Monday.

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI amend their deal to let OpenAI serve all its products across any cloud provider; Microsoft will no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI (OpenAI)
item.description: OpenAI : Microsoft and OpenAI amend their deal to let OpenAI serve all its products across any cloud provider; Microsoft will no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI   —  Amended agreement provides long-term clarity.  —  The rapid pace of innovation requires us to continue to evolve our partnership to benefit our customers and both companies.

item.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
item.description: Google and Kaggle launch a free intensive on building agents with Gemini and Vertex AI, scheduled for June 2026. Aimed at developers shifting from chat-completions to agentic workflows.

item.title: 1News: US vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' AI models
item.description: 1News reports US administration commitments to act against Chinese AI firms accused by Anthropic and others of model distillation. Follows DeepSeek V4 release; reframes the supply-chain governance debate from a NZ-Pacific viewpoint.

item.title: Open-source Dirac agent tops TerminalBench 2.0 at 65.2% using Gemini-3-flash-preview
item.description: Community-built open-source agent Dirac scored 65.2% on TerminalBench 2.0 with Gemini-3-flash-preview, beating Google's official 47.8% and closed-source leader Junie CLI's 64.3%. The project includes steps to avoid the benchmark gaming recently documented by researchers. HN discussion at 663 points.

item.title: China's NDRC orders Meta to cancel its $2B acquisition of AI agent startup Manus
item.description: China's National Development and Reform Commission ordered Meta to unwind its $2B acquisition of Chinese-founded AI agent startup Manus, citing tech sovereignty concerns after a months-long probe. Co-founders have been banned from leaving China. The deal, announced in December 2025, had already been partially integrated into Meta AI.

item.title: RNZ on Musk vs Altman trial: ongoing power struggle for OpenAI
item.description: RNZ frames the upcoming Musk vs Altman trial as the next public chapter of the OpenAI control fight, following the dismissal of fraud claims earlier this week. NZ pickup of the global story for domestic readers.

item.title: Lapsus$ hack of Mercor exposed 4TB of voice and biometric data from 40,000 AI contractors
item.description: A new analysis of the April Mercor breach (via LiteLLM supply chain attack) details how Lapsus$ stole 4TB of biometric data — including voice samples and ID documents — from 40,000 AI data contractors serving OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta. Six class-action lawsuits have been filed. Mercor disputes the claims.

item.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
item.description: Google DeepMind announces a strategic AI partnership with the South Korean government covering research collaboration, AI safety standards, and Gemini deployment in Korean public services. Part of the wider sovereign-AI push by frontier labs in Asia.

item.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
item.description: OpenAI and Microsoft formalise the next stage of their partnership: continued Azure compute commitment, expanded model access for Microsoft products, and revised IP arrangements. Signals continued tight coupling despite the Anthropic-Google deal.

item.title: Lancom joins AWS Anthropic reseller programme for Bedrock
item.description: NZ-based Lancom signs on as an AWS reseller for Anthropic Claude via Amazon Bedrock, leaning on a security/compliance positioning for enterprise customers. First explicit NZ channel partner on the Anthropic-via-Bedrock path.

item.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
item.description: Hugging Face publishes an integration guide for OpenAI's open-weight Privacy Filter PII redaction model in production web stacks. Covers latency tradeoffs, batching, and deployment on HF Inference Endpoints.

item.title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
item.description: OpenAI publishes Symphony, an open spec for multi-agent orchestration designed for use with Codex and other agent runtimes. Defines schemas for agent handoff, tool routing, and execution traces — direct competitor surface to Anthropic MCP and Google A2A.

item.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
item.description: OpenAI customer story: Berlin-based Choco deploys agents on the OpenAI stack to automate order intake and supplier matching across European food distributors. Concrete agentic deployment in a non-tech vertical.

item.title: NZ media flags Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview as "too dangerous", urges domestic guardrails
item.description: The Post frames Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview as a high-risk frontier release reaching New Zealand without local regulatory infrastructure. References the central-bank monitoring posture established last week and pushes for NZ-side governance rather than relying on US/AU regulators.

item.title: NZ MPs and AI in Parliament: which party uses it most, an MP running his own AI server, and Grok in Question Time
item.description: The Post investigates how New Zealand MPs use AI tools day-to-day in Parliament. The piece names the party with the highest AI adoption, identifies an MP self-hosting an LLM, and documents Grok being used to formulate questions during Question Time. First substantive look at AI usage inside the Beehive.

item.title: Altman says OpenAI "deeply sorry" for not flagging Canadian school shooter's ChatGPT use to police
item.description: Sam Altman issued a public apology for OpenAI's failure to escalate a Canadian school shooter's ChatGPT activity to law enforcement, calling the lapse a serious one and committing to revised internal escalation policies. The incident is fuelling fresh debate over consumer-AI duty-of-care obligations.
